title: The White Tiger
logline: A poor Indian driver from a village becomes entangled in his wealthy employers' corruption and moral compromises, ultimately breaking free from servitude through a desperate act of violence.
antagonistOrConflict: The caste system and economic servitude that traps the poor in cycles of exploitation

actBreaks.description: Balram arrives in Delhi and begins his transformation from village servant to city driver, establishing his new life of servitude in the capital.

validation.ruling: Scene 65 marks the major transition as Balram drives the family to Delhi in the Mitsubishi, representing his successful entry into their world and the irreversible move from village life to the corrupt urban environment that will transform him.

actBreaks.description: Balram reaches his breaking point and prepares for the violent act that will free him from his masters, gathering the broken bottle as his weapon.

validation.ruling: Scene 154 shows Ashok drunkenly confessing his dreams to Balram in an intimate moment under the mosquito net, representing the final humanization of Ashok before Balram's transformation into a killer, marking the end of their master-servant relationship.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated circular narrative structure, beginning and ending with Balram as a successful entrepreneur in Bangalore, then diving deep into the past to reveal how he escaped his servitude. The story functions as an extended confession, with present-day Balram narrating his transformation from village boy to murderer to businessman. The structure mirrors the cyclical nature of Indian society that the film critiques - servants replacing masters, corruption continuing in new forms. The narrative's circular design reinforces the theme that breaking free from one system often means entering another, though perhaps on different terms.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically in the final act, with rapid scene cuts building tension toward the murder. The middle section maintains steady momentum through Balram's moral education in Delhi, while the opening village sequences establish character and world with deliberate, observational pacing. Key acceleration points occur around the hit-and-run incident (scenes 106-111) and the final preparation for murder (scenes 175-189).

incitingIncident.description: Balram approaches the Stork's villa gate seeking work as a driver, lies about his experience to the hostile gatekeeper, then faints dramatically when the owners appear.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - AFTERNOON

validation.ruling: Scene 34 shatters Balram's ordinary world as he boldly approaches the Stork's villa seeking employment as a driver, using his village connection to gain entry and successfully positioning himself for the job that will transform his life from tea shop worker to driver in the wealthy family's world.

midpoint.description: Pinky drives erratically while drunk, swerving around obstacles as Ashok helps steer and they laugh together, until they hit a small figure in the road.

validation.ruling: Scene 107 is too passive - Balram is abandoned but doesn't make a crucial choice. Scene 108 is the true midpoint where Pinky drives drunk and hits a child, creating the point of no return that fundamentally shifts the story's stakes and sets up the corruption/cover-up that will define the rest of the narrative.

climax.description: Balram positions himself by the tire with the broken bottle, confirms the area is deserted, and tries to convince Ashok to step outside the car.

validation.ruling: Scene 189 shows aftermath, not the climactic confrontation itself. Scene 188 is the true climax where Balram positions himself with the broken bottle by the tire and tries to convince Ashok to step outside - this is the peak tension moment of the ultimate confrontation where Balram must act on his murderous plan.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / DELHI STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram, dressed as a maharaja, sits in the back seat of a speeding car while drunk Ashok and Pinky Madam laugh in front and command him to sing.

scenes.heading: EXT. RELIGIOUS TEMPLES - VARIOUS
scenes.summary: Various religious temples and believers are shown while Balram's voiceover discusses choosing which god to worship among millions of options.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Present-day Balram sits in his office under a chandelier, then watches TV news about a Chinese Premier's visit while typing an email.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. WHITE TIGER DRIVERS / FIRST FLOOR OFFICES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram comes downstairs to his taxi service where drivers and operators work, nods at young Dharam doing homework, and surveys his business empire.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE / GANGA RIVER - DAY [PAST]
scenes.summary: Water buffaloes are shown as Balram's voiceover identifies them as the best fed family members.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM'S FAMILY HOME; LAXMANGARH - DAY
scenes.summary: Granny Kusum surveys the village like a queen while Balram's voiceover introduces her.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEA SHOP / LAXMANGARH - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Kishan smashes coal at the tea shop where Granny forced him to work.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram's father strains to pull a heavy rickshaw while Granny takes every rupee he earns.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Images of poor village life are shown as Balram's voiceover explains that India is divided into two countries.

scenes.heading: EXT. GHATS AT A RIVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Balram walks along river ghats, observing white tourists doing yoga and locals bathing in the filthy water, illustrating India's divide between Light and Darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Balram prints a police wanted poster with his face, revealing through voiceover that police are searching for him due to an entrepreneurial act.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL IN LAXMANGARH - MORNING
scenes.summary: A school inspector tests students' English reading ability, and when others fail, young Balram successfully reads aloud and impresses the inspector who calls him a 'White Tiger.'
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram is identified as exceptional - a 'White Tiger' - establishing his intelligence and potential to break free from his circumstances.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH POND - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Balram playfully climbs on his father's back at a pond, teaching him English body parts as they laugh together.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM'S FAMILY HOME; LAXMANGARH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family sleeps together in piles while only Balram stays awake reading an English book in the darkness.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE / MAIN JUNCTION / TEA SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram and kids chase the Stork's white Ambassador car as it enters the village, and the Stork collects money from villagers while his son Mukesh assists.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL IN LAXMANGARH - MORNING
scenes.summary: While the teacher sleeps and kids play, only Balram reads a book until his brother Kishan appears at the door and tells him to gather his things.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEA SHOP; LAXMANGARH - DAY
scenes.summary: Kishan brings Balram to the tea shop and reveals he must work there because their father didn't pay the master, crushing Balram's dreams of going to Delhi.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram's education ends abruptly as he's forced into child labor, marking his entry into the cycle of servitude.

scenes.heading: INT. TEA SHOP; LAXMANGARH - LATER
scenes.summary: Kishan shows Balram how to break coal in the tea shop, telling him to imagine it's his skull if he doesn't like the work.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM’S FAMILY HOME; LAXMANGARH - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Coal-covered Balram eats dinner with his family while his ashamed father keeps his head down and Granny grins, telling him to eat.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE / MAIN JUNCTION - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram watches his tuberculosis-sick father cough up blood while pulling the rickshaw, and they must travel two days to find medical care.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. FREE HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: At the rundown hospital, Balram holds his dying father on the floor among sick people while Kishan tries unsuccessfully to bribe ward boys for a doctor.

scenes.heading: EXT. GHATS AT GANGA RIVER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram watches his father's corpse burn on a funeral pyre, sees his father's foot resist the flames, and faints from the overwhelming realization about the cycle of suffering.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram's father's death and his visceral reaction to seeing the body resist the flames crystallizes his understanding of the brutal cycle of poverty and rebirth.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARKET - DAY
scenes.summary: A butcher cuts a rooster's throat while other caged roosters watch indifferently, illustrating Balram's concept of the 'Rooster Coop.'

scenes.heading: EXT. DELHI - MORNING
scenes.summary: Crowds of working-class Indians move through Delhi streets as servants carry heavy loads and run errands.

scenes.heading: EXT. POSH HOME; DELHI - DAY
scenes.summary: A rickshaw driver delivers expensive furniture to a rich woman's home and receives payment worth far more than his salary.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARKET - DAY
scenes.summary: The driver faithfully returns all the cash to his store owner boss and bows respectfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. DELHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Wide shots of masses of poor people in Delhi illustrate Balram's voiceover about 99.9 percent being trapped in the Rooster Coop.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. TEA SHOP / LAXMANGARH - DAY
scenes.summary: Adult Balram still breaks coal in the tea shop while eavesdropping on customers, and he spots the Stork's new Mitsubishi SUV arriving in the village.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM’S FAMILY HOME - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram asks Granny for 300 rupees for driving lessons to become the Stork's second driver, but she refuses and insists he stay with his married brother Kishan.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM’S FAMILY HOME / LAXMANGARH VILLAGE - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Balram runs away from the village at sunrise carrying a small sack, hugging a water buffalo goodbye while Kishan chases him with a lunch box.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram breaks free from his family's control and village life, taking his first major step toward independence.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING BUS / LANDSCAPE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Balram sits ecstatically on a crowded bus with people, chickens, and goats as it travels toward Dhanbad.

scenes.heading: EXT. DHANBAD STREETS; VARIOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Dhanbad is revealed as a massive, chaotic coal city where blackened men and children carry stolen coal on bikes and their heads.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING TAXI / DHANBAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives a taxi joyfully until an old driver hits him and insults his caste, but then teaches him aggressive driving techniques after noting his skill.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram approaches the Stork's villa gate seeking work as a driver, lies about his experience to the hostile gatekeeper, then faints dramatically when the owners appear.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram's bold deception and theatrical fainting successfully gets him noticed by his future employers, marking his entry into their world.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING HONDA CITY / DHANBAD STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives Ashok and the Stork while Mukesh questions him aggressively about his caste, drinking habits, and potential for theft from the front seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The gatekeeper slams the gate on Balram while Mukesh makes a phone call, and Balram realizes they're investigating his family background.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE - DAY [IN BALRAM’S MIND]
scenes.summary: Balram imagines his family standing like a portrait outside their home while thugs in a car watch and report their location by phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. DHANBAD HOME - DAY [IN BALRAM’S MIND]
scenes.summary: In Balram's imagination, he stands tied against a wall and is shot dead by a thug.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLAGE; VARIOUS - DAY [IN BALRAM’S MIND]
scenes.summary: Balram imagines two thugs pulling out weapons and heading toward his family and home as punishment for any betrayal.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Present-day Balram stops typing at his laptop, disturbed as he explains how the Rooster Coop traps millions through family hostage systems.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DHANBAD - DAY [PAST]
scenes.summary: Ram Persad shows Balram to a small shared room and gives him a uniform, which fills Balram with awe and gratitude.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram steps out proudly in his uniform until the gatekeeper hits him and assigns him carpet cleaning and window washing duties instead of driving.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - DAY
scenes.summary: While Balram cleans a chandelier in the villa, he watches Ram Persad stroll about leisurely like a master.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DHANBAD - MORNING
scenes.summary: Balram spies on Ram Persad praying to Hindu idols and competitively prays louder and harder to avoid being accused of being a communist.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - SUNSET
scenes.summary: During dinner with other servants, Balram notices Ram Persad's absence and bad breath, then watches him sneak out of the gate suspiciously.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD; TERRACE - DAY
scenes.summary: While cleaning the terrace, Balram accidentally sees Pinky stretching Ashok's back, and when Ashok notices him, he fearfully looks away before being called over.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD; BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ashok and Pinky bring Balram into their bedroom where Pinky touches his hips and offers to adjust his alignment, while Ashok questions him and calls him 'half-baked.'

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram reflects on Ashok calling him 'half-baked' while watching the couple through their bedroom window and contemplating his incomplete education.

scenes.heading: EXT. DHANBAD STREETS / CYBER CAFE- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram walks through Dhanbad reading newspaper scraps and discovers a cyber cafe where he watches someone use the internet.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD; COURTYARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD; BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Balram pours disgusting water into the toilet, washes his hands, and discovers an expensive air freshener that costs twice his monthly salary.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD; DINING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Mukesh slaps money into Balram's hand for him to count, with Balram only allowed to keep 200 rupees.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram hands money to Kishan through the gate and tells him about Ashok's Christian wife from America, boasting that she talks to him as their number one servant.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DHANBAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram wakes to find Persad gone and discovers him chopping vegetables under a bare bulb outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Persad sneaks out the back of the home at sunrise while Balram follows him.

scenes.heading: EXT. DHANBAD STREETS / MOSQUE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Balram follows Persad to a mosque and realizes the number one driver is Muslim during Ramadan, then spies on Persad giving money to his family.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram discovers Persad's secret identity as a Muslim, giving him leverage that will change their dynamic

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Great Socialist arrives with bodyguards and staff, receiving a garland from Ashok while the Stork and Mukesh bow respectfully.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - LATER
scenes.summary: Balram eavesdrops as the Great Socialist demands two million rupees from the Stork for allowing them to take coal without paying taxes.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD; COURTYARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family discusses bypassing the Great Socialist by paying the opposing party in Delhi, with Pinky advocating for Ashok to go to Delhi as someone who understands international cities.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram spies on Mukesh giving Persad a bonus to drive the masters to Delhi.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM'S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS
scenes.summary: Balram sits pensively at his laptop, reflecting that he did something he wishes he didn't have to tell.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram threatens to expose Persad's Muslim identity to Mukesh, blackmailing him by calling him Mohammad and refusing to let him wash the car.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram actively betrays Persad using his secret, marking his first major moral compromise to advance his position

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S HOME IN DHANBAD - DUSK
scenes.summary: Balram watches Persad leave with his belongings through the gate, feeling guilty and wanting to apologize but not acting on it.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DHANBAD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Balram enters his room to find Persad's belongings gone except for Hindu idols, which he collects into a satchel.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. HIGHWAY TO DELHI / MOVING MITSUBISHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives the Mitsubishi to Delhi with Ashok, Pinky, and Mukesh, shouting at bus passengers about traveling in an air-conditioned car.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / VARIOUS DELHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram gawks at massive Delhi with its tree-lined boulevards and government buildings, slamming brakes and swerving around a homeless family in traffic.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / GURGAON - DUSK
scenes.summary: Balram looks up at the giant luxury apartments in Gurgaon at dusk.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram unloads suitcases and looks up at the towering apartment buildings that will be their new home.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram experiences his first elevator ride with a cart full of luggage, watching the numbers.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram enters the wealthy apartment and is startled by the expansive views, but Mukesh smacks him and orders him to place bags in specific rooms.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK'S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives the car down to the garage where all the servants live, including the drivers.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; SERVANT’S QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vitiligo-Lips shows Balram around the servant's quarters, explaining the facilities and pointing out their den of sin.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM'S ROOM; DELHI - LATER
scenes.summary: Balram writes his name on the wall in chalk, proud to have his very own room in Delhi.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK'S DELHI APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram counts the balconies of the apartment towers, amazed such buildings exist.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram opens the door for Ashok and Mukesh who carries a red leather bag, with Ashok stopping Balram from closing his door.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / DELHI STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives the brothers through Delhi traffic as they visit various government officials with the red bag.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL PARTY HEADQUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: The brothers meet with party members at National Party Headquarters with the red bag, then return to have Balram drive them to the President's House.

scenes.heading: INT. MITSUBISHI / BANK - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram watches the brothers put cash into a leather bag at the bank.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / DELHI STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram misses a turn and Mukesh smacks him, but Ashok defends Balram and touches his shoulder reassuringly.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / PRESIDENT’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram takes in the impressive President's House, then sees Ashok and Mukesh exit with the Minister's sidekick, with Ashok appearing sullen.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Ashok expresses disgust at giving bribes while driving past a Gandhi statue, calling democracy a joke, while Mukesh questions whether Pinky knows Ashok is staying in India.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION PLATFORM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Balram brings Mukesh dosa with potatoes removed at the train station, and Mukesh hits him for laughing when told not to drive without permission or try to have sex with the maid.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / DELHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Ashok smokes a joint while Balram drives, asks him to turn up music, leans over Balram to adjust it himself, and tells Balram not to tell anyone about the joint.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; TENNIS COURTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram gathers tennis balls while Ashok practices serves, complimenting Ashok's arms, but Ashok tells him to be quiet.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; GAME ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Ashok teaches Balram to play video games while Balram watches him transfixed by his happiness.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; KITCHEN / LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram finishes cleaning dishes then eavesdrops on Ashok and Pinky having sex through their bedroom door.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / MALL ENTRANCE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram drops Ashok and Pinky off at an impressive luxury mall entrance.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. MALL PARKING LOT / MITSUBISHI- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram stands with other drivers who pass around a murder magazine and make crude sexual comments about rich women, causing the drivers to laugh at Balram's nervousness.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; SERVANT’S QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram leaves the laughing servants with his bag and asks an old janitor for a room where he can be alone.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Balram finds a disgusting abandoned storage room with a cot and mosquito net and sets his bag down as his new home.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives Pinky and Ashok through countryside to Laxmangarh, with Pinky leaning over him to take photos and her breasts rubbing against him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S FAMILY’S HOME - LATER
scenes.summary: Balram drops Ashok and Pinky at the Buffalo's villa where family greets them with garlands, then realizes he must face his own family.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM’S FAMILY HOME - LATER
scenes.summary: Balram arrives at his family home where kids admire his car and uniform, but Kishan and Granny coldly confront him for not sending money since going to Delhi.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM’S FAMILY HOME - LATER
scenes.summary: Granny serves Balram chicken curry and announces she's found him a wife for marriage by year's end, while Balram protests he's not ready and refuses to eat meat.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / COUNTRY SIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives Ashok and Pinky through the countryside.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICE FIELD BY THE SIDE OF THE ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Ashok and Balram urinate by rice fields while Ashok complains about his family's questions, and Ashok discusses Bangalore's building boom and his idea to offer financial services.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Older Balram types at his laptop under maps of Bangalore, reflecting that things would have turned out differently.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / DELHI SCHOOL - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram sees school children in uniforms entering an impressive school while driving Ashok, who texts in the back seat.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Pinky scolds Balram for scratching his groin and having a filthy uniform while serving tea, then later calls him to sit and talk with her.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET MARKET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram buys toothpaste, black shoes, and a shirt like Ashok wears from vendors in a busy street market.

scenes.heading: INT. DELHI APARTMENT; SERVANT’S QUARTERS; BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram brushes his teeth repeatedly in the bathroom mirror, unable to remove paan stains despite scrubbing harder, while reflecting on his father's failure to teach him basic hygiene.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Balram wakes to find cockroaches crawling on his mosquito net and calmly crushes one between his fingers.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: A match burns a wanted poster showing Balram's face with 'WANTED FOR MURDER' written on it.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT [PAST]
scenes.summary: Balram arrives at Ashok's apartment dressed as a maharaja with balloons and pizza boxes to celebrate Pinky's birthday.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONNAUGHT PLACE PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Still dressed as a maharaja, Balram watches a disco club from the parking lot while other drivers mock his costume and living situation.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives while watching Ashok and Pinky make out in the rear-view mirror, touching himself until Ashok notices and stops, leading to an argument between the couple about returning to New York.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / INTERSECTION - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At a red light, Pinky buys Buddha statues from a beggar child named Khushnuma, then orders Balram out of the car after he tells the child not to touch it, taking over the driving herself.
scenes.turningPointNote: Pinky takes control of the car, setting up the fatal accident

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pinky drives erratically while drunk, swerving around obstacles as Ashok helps steer and they laugh together, until they hit a small figure in the road.
scenes.turningPointNote: The fatal car accident occurs

scenes.heading: EXT. MITSUBISHI / STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Balram discovers the lump in the road is a child while Ashok wants to call police and take the victim to hospital, but Balram warns this will get Pinky in trouble.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Balram drives away from the accident scene while Pinky becomes hysterical in the backseat, screaming that she killed a kid and demanding they return to check if the child is alive.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; GARAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Balram scrubs blood from the car's tires and fender in the garage, finding bloody fabric while Ashok questions what the child was doing in the street and Balram callously dismisses the victim.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram lies in bed smiling with satisfaction at having served his master well in a difficult moment, but his smile fades when he sees the Buddha statue.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Balram burns incense inside the Mitsubishi and stares at the car under the morning sun.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mukesh arrives with the Stork and a lawyer, treating Balram unusually well by inviting him to sit on the sofa and forcing him to chew paan while discussing a confession that will frame Balram for the accident.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram is set up to take the fall for the hit-and-run

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK'S DELHI APART; HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Balram exits the apartment and waits for the elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK'S DELHI APART; ELEVATOR
scenes.summary: Balram rides down in the elevator with his heart sinking.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram walks outside where the sun blinds him, stares up at the luxury towers looming over him, then hides in weeds curled up trying to save himself.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Older Balram sits in lotus position under a chandelier, opens his eyes and tells the camera that thinking about this makes him angry enough to cut a rich man's throat.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - NIGHT [PAST]
scenes.summary: Balram sits curled up in his mosquito net, reflecting that he never considered he had options like telling the truth or running away.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LIQUOR STORE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram pushes through a crowd to buy cheap whiskey, drinks it, and squats with other drunks while observing homeless people and reflecting on being trapped in the system.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram watches Ashok smoking a joint outside the apartment building while contemplating accepting what the family will do to him and his family.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram sits alone in the dark garage reflecting that escaping his situation would take a freak of nature - a White Tiger.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Vitiligo-Lips finds Balram hiding in his mosquito net while the phone rings constantly, telling him his boss has been looking for him frantically.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram massages the Stork's legs when Ashok and Pinky return, and Pinky sits on the floor to personally tell Balram they no longer need his confession since no one reported the accident.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram learns he's no longer needed to take the fall, but remains trapped

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: At the train station, Mukesh tells Balram he's keeping the signed confession in his jacket pocket forever before boarding the departing train.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD DELHI - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Balram walks through Old Delhi observing poverty around him, watching a man boil tea over a blue flame, seeing monkeys on power cables, and angrily rejecting an old beggar woman's pleas for money.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Pinky wakes Balram in his dark room and asks him to drive her, with her suitcase visible.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives Pinky alone while she stares at him in the rear-view mirror and speaks cryptically about doors being open, though she appears to be talking to herself.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / AIRPORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drops Pinky at the airport where she pushes a brown envelope of money through his window before leaving for her flight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Pinky leaves India permanently, abandoning Ashok

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Ashok angrily confronts Balram about driving Pinky to the airport without telling him, repeatedly slapping Balram until Balram shoves him off into the sofa.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram waits anxiously in his room for two days for his master to call him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK'S DELHI APARTMENT - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Balram stares at the giant luxury towers at sunset.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram paces in his room wondering if Ashok has returned to America without telling him.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram finds Ashok drunk and disheveled, helps him when he vomits, and tends to him by wiping his mouth clean with a wet towel.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; VARIOUS - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram prepares a careful drink for Ashok but finds him passed out, slaps him twice without waking him, then sits down to drink it himself and puts his feet on the table.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; BALCONY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram enjoys the view from Ashok's balcony with a glass of whiskey, savoring his moment as master.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARKET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram and Ashok walk through a crowded market together, laughing and talking.

scenes.heading: INT. POOR MAN’S CAFE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram takes Ashok to a poor man's cafe where Ashok loves the food and says he wants to eat only this kind of food now, while Balram lies to cheer him up about Pinky.

scenes.heading: INT. ROADSIDE TEMPLE - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Balram and Ashok pray together at a roadside temple, putting money between statues and receiving tikas from a priest while Balram reassures Ashok that Pinky will return.

scenes.heading: EXT. DELHI - VARIOUS
scenes.summary: Images of Delhi are shown.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Mukesh arrives at the train station, side-hugs Ashok, then shoves his luggage into Balram's chest and dismissively orders him to get the car.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: While preparing lunch, Balram spies on Mukesh telling Ashok that Pinky wants a divorce, with Mukesh comforting Ashok and discouraging him from calling her.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; ELEVATOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Balram pinches his hand repeatedly in the elevator, questioning why he remains in servitude.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; GARAGE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram walks through the garage reflecting that the desire to be a servant has been bred into his blood and hammered into his skull.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Balram counts his money from under the bed and talks to himself and the Buddha statue, calculating that Pinky's payment was far less than what she truly owed him for the confession he signed.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram begins to feel entitled to more money and resentful of his treatment

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Vitiligo explains to Balram that drivers work until their eyes fail, then either buy a shack in a slum if they save money or end up with nothing if they're fired or have an accident.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK'S DELHI APART - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram watches Ashok smoking and playing video games from the balcony, resolving not to let his fate be a shack in a slum.

scenes.heading: EXT. MECHANIC SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: A mechanic hands Balram an invoice and Balram pays him a small amount of money as he begins learning how drivers cheat their masters.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram gives Ashok a phony repair invoice and receives a wad of cash in return.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION / STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram pumps gas into a can at a gas station, then later pours it into a taxi and receives payment from the driver.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / DELHI STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram operates as an illegal taxi driver, picking up customers and collecting cash while narrating about gaining confidence in the business.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS OF DELHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram speeds through Delhi streets while reflecting in voiceover that looking at the cash made him feel rage, not guilt.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives drunk, drinking whiskey and singing, then talks to an imaginary Ashok in the backseat, cursing and insulting him.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; GARAGE; BALRAM’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ashok, drunk in Balram's room, confronts him about his whereabouts but believes Balram's lie about praying at a temple, then shares his regrets about never pursuing music.

scenes.heading: INT. FIVE-STAR BANGALORE HOTEL - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Present-day Balram sits in a five-star hotel bar watching news of a Chinese premier's arrival while reflecting on his transformation from servant to wealthy businessman.

scenes.heading: INT. MITSUBISHI / TRAFFIC JAM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a traffic jam, Balram eavesdrops as Mukesh discusses bribing politicians until elections while berating Balram for giving money to a legless beggar.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram serves drinks to Ashok and Mukesh while spying on them through a mirror, believing he lip-reads Mukesh saying 'replacement' about him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Ashok hands Balram the red bag to hold while retrieving his phone, and Balram discovers it contains stacks of money worth at least 700,000 rupees.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram discovers the massive amount of cash in the red bag, escalating his temptation and desperation

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. [MOVING] MITSUBISHI / PARLIAMENT HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives Ashok to government buildings where guards watch as Ashok carries the red bag inside for meetings.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. [MOVING] MITSUBISHI / DELHI STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives through Delhi at night, fixated on the illuminated red bag near Ashok while observing the city's poor sleeping in the streets.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / DELHI BANK - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram watches from the car as Ashok withdraws cash from an ATM and adds it to the red bag.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / TRAFFIC - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Balram hallucinates that a rickshaw driver is his father telling him stealing the bag wouldn't be theft since Ashok steals from ordinary people through bribes.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. [MOVING] MITSUBISHI / SHERATON HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ashok takes the red bag into the Sheraton Hotel, telling Balram he'll be a couple hours before Balram drives away.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. TRAIN STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram enters a train station and contemplates escape destinations while pigeons and a growling dog seem to sense his plan to steal the red bag.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEMPLE ON HILL; LAXMANGARH - MORNING [BALRAM’S MIND]
scenes.summary: Balram imagines his brother Kishan being beaten to death and dumped next to his dead wife at a temple.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALRAM’S FAMILY HOME; LAXMANGARH - MORNING [BALRAM’S
scenes.summary: Balram envisions his entire family being massacred - uncles, aunts, children shot and stabbed, including his grandmother being stabbed to death.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A barking dog pulls Balram out of his nightmare visions of his family's murder.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives while staring at Ashok and the red bag in the rear-view mirror as Ashok talks on his phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram tells Ashok he wants to smash his skull and steal his money, but Ashok misinterprets this as homesickness and gives him money for a trip home.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram visits a slum where he watches children play in sewage and squats next to a man defecating in daylight, both of them laughing.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram drives through celebrations of the Great Socialist's election victory while Ashok angrily discusses on the phone how they backed the wrong political side.
scenes.turningPointNote: The political landscape shifts as the Great Socialist wins, threatening Ashok's bribery arrangements

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / SHERATON HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram opens the car door for the Great Socialist as she exits the Sheraton Hotel surrounded by guards and supporters chanting for socialism.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram drives the Great Socialist and her associate who demand 40 lacs (4 million rupees) from Ashok, revealing the massive bribe amount.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSTRUCTION SITE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Balram finds a wrench at a construction site and practices swinging it into concrete, imagining using it as a weapon.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE OUTSIDE BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram's young relative Dharam arrives with a letter from his grandmother demanding money and threatening to expose Balram to his master if he refuses an arranged marriage.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram's grandmother speaks to the camera threatening to send him a bride or expose him to his master if he doesn't send money and return for his arranged wedding.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE OUTSIDE BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Balram slaps Dharam hard twice, then is shocked by his own violence and retreats to a corner trembling as he breaks apart emotionally.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Ashok gives Balram a day off and meets Dharam, while Balram realizes this unusual generosity means something is wrong.

scenes.heading: EXT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram spies on Ashok greeting a dark-skinned servant in the lobby and realizes this man is his replacement.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram discovers he's being replaced, eliminating his last reason to hesitate about stealing

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL ZOO - VARIOUS
scenes.summary: At the zoo, Balram shows Dharam animals before they encounter a white tiger, which transfixes Balram as he experiences visions of his father's death and suffering.
scenes.turningPointNote: The white tiger serves as a powerful symbol of Balram's rare potential to break free from his caste

scenes.heading: EXT. LAXMANGARH VILLAGE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Young Balram stands on a cliff looking at a river in his village.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; GARAGE -
scenes.summary: Balram smashes a Johnnie Walker bottle against a column, creating a weapon with long, cruel, clawlike glass shards.

scenes.heading: INT. ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT; BALRAM’S ROOM/GARAGE - VARIOUS
scenes.summary: Balram performs final rituals with the car, places the broken bottle and his belongings inside, gives Dharam money, and drives away for the last time.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MITSUBISHI / ASHOK’S DELHI APARTMENT - NIGHT [RAIN]
scenes.summary: Balram picks up Ashok and the red bag, correctly anticipating their destination is the Sheraton Hotel without being told.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - NIGHT [RAIN]
scenes.summary: Balram drives through the rain while Ashok discusses Pinky's call and his decision to stay in India, then Balram tells a story about Buddha awakening while others sleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. SECLUDED DELHI STREET - NIGHT [RAIN]
scenes.summary: Balram turns onto a secluded road in the rain.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING MITSUBISHI - CONTINUOUS [RAIN]
scenes.summary: Balram stops the car claiming wheel trouble, picks up the broken bottle, and exits into the rain while Ashok remains inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. SECLUDED DELHI STREET - CONTINUOUS [RAIN]
scenes.summary: Balram positions himself by the tire with the broken bottle, confirms the area is deserted, and tries to convince Ashok to step outside the car.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING MITSUBISHI / SECLUDED DELHI STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram floors the accelerator and speeds away, shouting wildly, then laughs at the red bag before becoming scared as he realizes he has killed Ashok.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram murders Ashok and steals the money, the climactic act that transforms him from servant to criminal

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the train station with the red bag, Balram waits anxiously for a train while seeing a homeless family, then decides to return for Dharam.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S ROOM; DELHI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram finds Dharam awake in his room, takes his hand, and they leave together.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVING TRAIN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Balram and Dharam travel by train with Balram clutching the red bag and holding Dharam's hand while staring anxiously out the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL TRAIN STATION - MORNING
scenes.summary: Balram and Dharam wait at a rural train station and board another train to continue their journey.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING TRAIN IN LANDSCAPE - DUSK
scenes.summary: The train travels through the landscape at dusk with Balram holding the red bag as Dharam sleeps on his shoulder.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. HOTEL IN BANGALORE - VARIOUS [PAST]
scenes.summary: In a Bangalore hotel, Balram grows a beard and stays in bed for four weeks, unable to eat, while Dharam sleeps on the floor and watches over him.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANGALORE STREET; BARBER - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram gets his hair and beard trimmed at a barber shop, transforming his appearance as he prepares to become a business entrepreneur.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANGALORE; TECH CITIES; VARIOUS - VARIOUS
scenes.summary: Balram and Dharam explore Bangalore's modern tech cities and impressive campuses that resemble Silicon Valley.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MOVING RICKSHAW / ELECTRONICS CITY - VARIOUS
scenes.summary: Balram and Dharam ride through Electronics City observing tech companies and SUVs dropping off workers, as Balram identifies his business opportunity in transportation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram discovers his business model by observing the outsourcing industry's transportation needs

scenes.heading: INT. BANGALORE POLICE STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Balram bribes the Assistant Commissioner of Police with a leather bag of money, securing his cooperation for future favors.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANGALORE TAXI SERVICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Police raid a competing taxi service and arrest drivers for expired licenses while Balram watches approvingly, having eliminated his competition through bribery.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT [PRESENT]
scenes.summary: Three years later, a now pot-bellied and older Balram oversees his successful White Tiger Drivers business with thirty drivers and twenty-six air-conditioned vehicles, worth fifteen times what he borrowed from Mr. Ashok.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram sits at his laptop showing his White Tiger Drivers website and reveals to the camera that he now calls himself 'Ashok.'

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram hands out paychecks to a line of drivers while Dharam does bookkeeping, explaining that he treats them as employees with contracts rather than servants or family.

scenes.heading: EXT. SITE OF ACCIDENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a hit-and-run accident scene where a 12-year-old boy lies dead in the mud, Balram tells police he is the owner and takes responsibility, declaring the blood is on his hands rather than his driver's.

scenes.heading: INT. BANGALORE POLICE STATION; OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram and the dead boy's angry teenage brother wait in a police station office as the Assistant Commissioner enters with two policemen.

scenes.heading: INT. DEAD BOY’S HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: In the dead boy's home, Balram offers the grieving parents forty thousand rupees from a brown envelope and proposes to help their surviving son by giving him a job as a driver.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANGALORE CAFE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: At a cafe, Balram reads a newspaper headline about seventeen family members murdered in a North Indian village while Dharam does homework, then asks Dharam if he thinks about his parents.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANGALORE LUXURY HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: With his own police escort, Balram meets Chinese Premier Wen Jiabao at a luxury hotel, shaking his hand for press photos while pitching real estate opportunities for American companies coming to Bangalore.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S MOVING CAR; BANGALORE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Driving his BMW through Bangalore at night, Balram reflects that he misses his former master and didn't deserve his fate, wishing he had killed the Mongoose instead.

scenes.heading: INT. BALRAM’S OFFICE; WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Back at his laptop where the story began, Balram types his email and reflects that even if caught, he would never say he made a mistake because it was worthwhile to know what it means not to be a servant.
scenes.turningPointNote: Balram's final philosophical statement completes his transformation and justifies his actions, bringing the narrative full circle to where it began.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. WHITE TIGER DRIVERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Balram addresses his drivers directly, explaining that unlike Hindi films where murderers have nightmares of their victims, the real nightmare is not killing your master and remaining a servant forever.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
